on-error-propagate.doc:name: 400 APIKit Bad Request
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles malformed or invalid request payloads and parameters from APIKit validation

set-payload.value: #[output application/json --- {code: 400, message: 'Bad Request', description: (error.description default 'The request body is missing required fields or contains invalid values.'), timestamp: now() as String}]
set-payload.doc:name: Build 400 Response
set-payload.doc:description: Returns a standard 400 JSON error response for bad APIKit requests

on-error-propagate.doc:name: 404 APIKit Not Found
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles requests to API resources or employee records that do not exist

set-payload.value: #[output application/json --- {code: 404, message: 'Not Found', description: (error.description default 'The requested resource or employee record does not exist.'), timestamp: now() as String}]
set-payload.doc:name: Build 404 Response
set-payload.doc:description: Returns a standard 404 JSON error response for resource not found

on-error-propagate.doc:name: 405 Method Not Allowed
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles HTTP methods not supported by the Employee Details API specification

set-payload.value: #[output application/json --- {code: 405, message: 'Method Not Allowed', description: (error.description default 'The HTTP method used is not allowed for this resource.'), timestamp: now() as String}]
set-payload.doc:name: Build 405 Response
set-payload.doc:description: Returns a standard 405 JSON error response for unsupported HTTP methods

on-error-propagate.doc:name: 406 Not Acceptable
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles requests where the requested media type cannot be served by the API

set-payload.value: #[output application/json --- {code: 406, message: 'Not Acceptable', description: (error.description default 'The requested media type is not supported by this API.'), timestamp: now() as String}]
set-payload.doc:name: Build 406 Response
set-payload.doc:description: Returns a standard 406 JSON error response for unacceptable media types

on-error-propagate.doc:name: 415 Unsupported Media Type
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles requests with a Content-Type not supported by the Employee Details API

set-payload.value: #[output application/json --- {code: 415, message: 'Unsupported Media Type', description: (error.description default 'The request Content-Type is not supported. Use application/json.'), timestamp: now() as String}]
set-payload.doc:name: Build 415 Response
set-payload.doc:description: Returns a standard 415 JSON error response for unsupported content types

on-error-propagate.doc:name: 400 HTTP Bad Request
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles 400 Bad Request errors from outbound HTTP calls

set-payload.value: #[output application/json --- {code: 400, message: 'Bad Request', description: (error.description default 'The HTTP request was malformed or contained invalid parameters.'), timestamp: now() as String}]
set-payload.doc:name: Build 400 HTTP Response
set-payload.doc:description: Returns a standard 400 JSON error response for HTTP bad request errors

on-error-propagate.doc:name: 401 HTTP Unauthorized
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles 401 Unauthorized errors from outbound HTTP calls

set-payload.value: #[output application/json --- {code: 401, message: 'Unauthorized', description: (error.description default 'Authentication is required to access this resource.'), timestamp: now() as String}]
set-payload.doc:name: Build 401 Response
set-payload.doc:description: Returns a standard 401 JSON error response for unauthorized HTTP requests

on-error-propagate.doc:name: 403 HTTP Forbidden
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles 403 Forbidden errors from outbound HTTP calls

set-payload.value: #[output application/json --- {code: 403, message: 'Forbidden', description: (error.description default 'You do not have permission to access this resource.'), timestamp: now() as String}]
set-payload.doc:name: Build 403 Response
set-payload.doc:description: Returns a standard 403 JSON error response for forbidden HTTP requests

on-error-propagate.doc:name: 404 HTTP Not Found
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles 404 Not Found errors from outbound HTTP calls

set-payload.value: #[output application/json --- {code: 404, message: 'Not Found', description: (error.description default 'The requested HTTP resource was not found.'), timestamp: now() as String}]
set-payload.doc:name: Build 404 HTTP Response
set-payload.doc:description: Returns a standard 404 JSON error response for HTTP not found errors

on-error-propagate.doc:name: 408 HTTP Timeout
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles request timeout errors from outbound HTTP calls

set-payload.value: #[output application/json --- {code: 408, message: 'Request Timeout', description: (error.description default 'The HTTP request timed out. Please try again later.'), timestamp: now() as String}]
set-payload.doc:name: Build 408 Response
set-payload.doc:description: Returns a standard 408 JSON error response for HTTP timeout errors

on-error-propagate.doc:name: 503 HTTP Connectivity
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles HTTP connectivity failures when the downstream service is unreachable

set-payload.value: #[output application/json --- {code: 503, message: 'Service Unavailable', description: (error.description default 'Unable to connect to the downstream HTTP service. Please try again later.'), timestamp: now() as String}]
set-payload.doc:name: Build 503 HTTP Connectivity Response
set-payload.doc:description: Returns a standard 503 JSON error response for HTTP connectivity failures

on-error-propagate.doc:name: 404 FTP File Not Found
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles the case where the employees.json data file does not exist on the FTP server

set-payload.value: #[output application/json --- {code: 404, message: 'Not Found', description: (error.description default 'The employee data file does not exist on the FTP server.'), timestamp: now() as String}]
set-payload.doc:name: Build 404 FTP Response
set-payload.doc:description: Returns a 404 JSON error response when the employee data file is missing on the FTP server

on-error-propagate.doc:name: 400 FTP Illegal Path
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles invalid or illegal FTP path errors when reading or writing employee data

set-payload.value: #[output application/json --- {code: 400, message: 'Bad Request', description: (error.description default 'The FTP file path is invalid or illegal.'), timestamp: now() as String}]
set-payload.doc:name: Build 400 FTP Path Response
set-payload.doc:description: Returns a 400 JSON error response for invalid FTP file paths

on-error-propagate.doc:name: 503 FTP Connectivity
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles FTP server connection failures when the FTP server is unreachable

set-payload.value: #[output application/json --- {code: 503, message: 'Service Unavailable', description: (error.description default 'Unable to connect to the FTP server. Please verify FTP configuration and try again.'), timestamp: now() as String}]
set-payload.doc:name: Build 503 FTP Connectivity Response
set-payload.doc:description: Returns a 503 JSON error response when the FTP server cannot be reached

on-error-propagate.doc:name: 503 FTP Retry Exhausted
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles the case where all FTP retry attempts have been exhausted

set-payload.value: #[output application/json --- {code: 503, message: 'Service Unavailable', description: (error.description default 'FTP operation failed after exhausting all retry attempts.'), timestamp: now() as String}]
set-payload.doc:name: Build 503 FTP Retry Response
set-payload.doc:description: Returns a 503 JSON error response when FTP retry attempts are exhausted

on-error-propagate.doc:name: 404 App Not Found
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles APP:NOT_FOUND raised when a requested employee record does not exist in the data source

set-payload.value: #[output application/json --- {code: 404, message: 'Not Found', description: (error.description default 'Data not found'), timestamp: now() as String}]
set-payload.doc:name: Build 404 App Response
set-payload.doc:description: Returns a 404 JSON error response when the requested employee data is not found

on-error-propagate.doc:name: 409 App Conflict
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Handles APP:CONFLICT raised when a POST request attempts to create an employee that already exists

set-payload.value: #[output application/json --- {code: 409, message: 'Conflict', description: (error.description default 'Record already exists'), timestamp: now() as String}]
set-payload.doc:name: Build 409 App Response
set-payload.doc:description: Returns a 409 JSON error response when a duplicate employee record is detected

on-error-propagate.doc:name: 500 Internal Server Error
on-error-propagate.doc:description: Catch-all handler for unexpected errors not covered by specific error type handlers

set-payload.value: #[output application/json --- {code: 500, message: 'Internal Server Error', description: (error.description default 'An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later or contact support.'), timestamp: now() as String}]
set-payload.doc:name: Build 500 Response
set-payload.doc:description: Returns a standard 500 JSON error response for any unexpected unhandled error
